skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables
stem: Maya is working this summer at a community garden. She earned $9 per hour for the first 12 hours she worked this week. Because of her dedication, her supervisor raised her pay to $12 per hour for the rest of the week. Maya saves 80% of her earnings each week. What is the least number of hours she must work the rest of the week to save at least $240 for the week?
rationale: Choice D is correct. Maya earns $9 × 12 = $108 for the first 12 hours. Let x be additional hours at $12/hr; total earnings = 12x + 108. Saving 80%: 0.8(12x + 108) ≥ 240, so 12x + 108 ≥ 300, giving 12x ≥ 192 and x ≥ 16. So the least is 16 hours. Other choices overestimate the hours needed.
